title: Multi-surface distribution: extract knowledge layer + ship as CLI + Claude Code plugin
description:
Inspired by EtienneLescot/n8n-as-code (1049 stars, 4 months old, daily bot regen). Today mediawiki-mcp-server ships as stdio MCP only. The pattern to copy: extract knowledge/logic into a shared package, then ship as MCP server + CLI tool + Claude Code plugin (via marketplace install).

Scope (research bead, not implementation):
1. Inventory what would live in the shared knowledge layer â€” likely: page-resolution helpers, link-audit logic, terminology/translation rules, MediaWiki API client, formatting converters (markdown â†” wikitext)
2. Sketch CLI surface â€” candidates: 'mw publish <file>', 'mw audit-links [--section]', 'mw stale-pages', 'mw search <terms>', 'mw replace <pattern> <replacement>'. The publish-docs skill currently shells out manually; CLI extraction would let it ship as a real tool.
3. Sketch Claude Code plugin manifest â€” reference EtienneLescot/n8n-as-code/.claude-plugin/ for working manifest.json format. Plugin packaging would let publish-docs + lmwtfy + bid-intel skills ship as installable rather than hand-rolled.
4. Decide bundling pattern: knowledge bundled WITH server (Memtrace pattern, see memtrace-public sift 05-05-2026) vs separate package consumed across surfaces (n8n-as-code pattern). Pick before any code change.

Reference: ~/Documents/remote-v/AI-Knowledge/n8n-as-code - Etienne Lescot.md
Reference: ~/Documents/remote-v/Career/SoMe-Content/Drafts/Substack - Multi-Surface MCP Distribution.md

Definition of done for THIS bead: written design doc in repo root committing to one bundling pattern + CLI surface skeleton + .claude-plugin/ manifest stub. No implementation.

title: wiki recent: rcstart/rcend swap â€” same time-window semantic bug as history
description:
Same MediaWiki `rcdir=older`-default semantic mismatch as fixed in `GetRevisions` / `GetUserContributions`. The CLI exposes `--start` / `--end` with user-friendly bound semantics (start = lower, end = upper), but `GetRecentChanges` in `wiki/history.go` maps them directly onto `rcstart` / `rcend`, which under `rcdir=older` mean the opposite (rcstart = newer bound, rcend = older bound).

## Suspected affected callers

- `wiki recent` CLI command (`cmd/wiki/recent.go`) â€” verify whether it passes --start/--end through.
- The `mediawiki_get_recent_changes` MCP tool.

## Suspected location

`wiki/history.go` â€” `GetRecentChanges`, lines around 39-45 (the `args.Start` / `args.End` block that sets `rcstart` / `rcend`).

## Why this matters

After mediawiki-mcp-server-qzd ships, "last edited" and "user contributions" lookups are correct but "recent changes" still silently returns the wrong window. The bug surface remains.

## Fix shape

Mirror the qzd fix: swap the param mapping so caller's Start â†’ rcend and caller's End â†’ rcstart. Add equivalent param-mapping assertions to `TestGetRecentChanges_*` tests (audit existing coverage first).
acceptance_criteria:
- `wiki recent --start YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z` returns only changes whose timestamp is >= start
- Same for --end
- Same for the `mediawiki_get_recent_changes` MCP tool
- Test in `wiki/history_test.go` asserts the swapped mapping for `GetRecentChanges`

title: wiki history --start filter is ignored â€” returns full history regardless
description:
The `--start` flag on `wiki history <page>` does not constrain the returned revisions to the requested time window. Output includes revisions from years before the start timestamp.

## Repro (observed 15-05-2026, build from main@f1059fe)

```
~/go/bin/wiki history "360Â° Integration with MS Teams - Implementation guidelines" \
  --start 2026-04-17T00:00:00Z --limit 50
```

Expected: only revisions on or after 2026-04-17.
Actual: 50 revisions returned, spanning 2026-02-11 back to 2021-06-02. The 4-week window contained zero revisions, but instead of returning an empty/short result, the CLI falls back to unfiltered history.

Reproduced on a second page (`360Â° Integration with MS Teams`) â€” same behaviour: revisions from 2023 returned despite a 2026-04-17 start.

## Suspected location

Likely `cmd/wiki/history.go` â€” the --start flag is parsed but probably not passed into the MediaWiki API call (or the API call doesn't include `rvstart` / equivalent param). The MediaWiki API itself supports time-bounded revision queries; the CLI plumbing is the suspect.

## Why this matters

The CLI is otherwise the right answer for "who edited X recently?" â€” and the --start filter is the obvious shape for that question. Without it, callers have to post-filter in shell or grep the dates, which defeats the typed-exit-code + --json pipeline story.
acceptance_criteria:
- `wiki history <page> --start YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z` returns only revisions whose timestamp is >= start
- Same for `--end` (verify symmetric behaviour while in the area)
- If the window contains zero revisions, the CLI returns an empty result (or a clear "no revisions in window" message) â€” NOT a fallback to full history
- Add a smoke test in `cmd/wiki/history_test.go` covering both branches
notes: Fixed via param-mapping swap in GetRevisions + GetUserContributions. rvstart/rvend (and ucstart/ucend) now correctly mapped under default rcdir=older semantics. Acceptance: --start lower bound + --end upper bound work, zero-window returns empty (no full-history fallback), regression test added (TestGetRevisions_EmptyTimeWindow). Verified live against wiki.software-innovation.com: 4-week window on '360Â° Integration with MS Teams - Implementation guidelines' surfaces 2026-05-11 revision that the buggy version hid. Follow-up bead k90 covers the same fix shape for GetRecentChanges.

title: Plugin v0.1.0 release: tag + install test
description: After at least one skill lands (wiki-publish at minimum), tag the plugin release: bump version in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json + plugin.json to 0.1.0, tag the repo, validate /plugin marketplace add olgasafonova/mediawiki-mcp-server end-to-end. Depends on wiki-publish skill being merged. Per the existing release workflow rules (matrix builds, gh release upload, no softprops), the plugin needs no new CI â€” the manifest is discovered by Claude Code from the default branch at install time.

title: Write wiki-publish skill (first plugin skill)
description: First end-to-end skill in .claude-plugin/skills/. Calls 'wiki publish <file> --json' and reports the revision URL + diff URL. Validates the plugin install path before any other skill is written. See MULTI-SURFACE-DISTRIBUTION.md for the skill contract (every skill shells out to the wiki CLI; no Go re-implementation). DoD: skills/wiki-publish/SKILL.md committed, plugin still loads via /plugin marketplace add olgasafonova/mediawiki-mcp-server, one real publish proven end-to-end.

title: Add 'wiki stale-pages' and 'wiki similar' CLI subcommands
description: Two missing CLI surfaces flagged in MULTI-SURFACE-DISTRIBUTION.md. MCP tools mediawiki_get_stale_pages and mediawiki_find_similar_pages exist; CLI does not wrap them. Add: 'wiki stale-pages [--days N] [--category X]' wrapping GetStalePages; 'wiki similar <page>' wrapping FindSimilarPages. Both follow the existing cobra command pattern in cmd/wiki/ and support --json + --quiet. Required before wiki-audit skill ships.

title: Refactor tools/handlers.go to green (cc reduction)
description: tools/handlers.go is at Code Health 7.64 (yellow). Three pre-existing complex methods drive it: register (cc=43), registerByName (cc=43), logExecution (cc=24). Path to green (9.1+): split each by category (search/read/write/etc). ~15 small functions replacing 3 monsters. ~250-line diff. CodeScene business case: target 9.1 (top 5%) yields 17-26% time reduction, 26-35% defect reduction at 90% CI. Not architecturally risky â€” preserves type-safe dispatch, just regroups it. No new dependencies. Tests cover the dispatch logic so refactor is verifiable. Spun out of claude-code-config-0x2.1 (rationale parameter work, 13-05-2026).
close_reason: Refactored: registerByName switch replaced by methodRegistrars map + grouped by category. tools/handlers.go score 7.64 -> 9.53 (yellow -> green).

title: Add tests for wiki/dedup.go
description: 240 LOC deduplication logic with zero test coverage. Found by desloppify portfolio scan. Contains request deduplication for MediaWiki API calls.
